Petitiin For Order Consveying Real Property And Resulting Trust; Undue Influnce; Financial Abuse Of An Elder;Etc.
Off Calendar Jun-17-2013 Continued To Sep-16-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Mills for Ronald Coleman and Margaret Coleman (Petitioners).

The Court stated that the Probate Court is the proper Court to handle this matter, and that this is an 850 Petition and that it is proper in the Probate Court. Mr. Mills stated that he is not willing to participate in Pro Bono Mediation. The Petition for Order Conveying Real Property and Resulting Trust, Undue Influence, etc. was continued to September 16, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mann.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Mills for Ronald Coleman and Margaret Coleman. Winnie Kwong for Thomas Coleman (first named Executor).

Ms. Kwong stated that their position is that there is no need for probate, that there is no real property in the Estate and that the property was left in joint tenancy. Counsel stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter and upon returning informed the Court that the amended petitions filed on September 5, 2012 and the second amended petition filed on November 30, 2012 are withdrawn, that Thomas Coleman will resubmit Letters, Duties and Liabilities and an order for probate. When informed by the Court that the petition for probate would not be approved today due to procedural defects, the Parties spoke with the Probate Examiner and upon returning Mr. Mills stated that he will withdraw the original petition filed on filed on July 5, 2012 and file an 850 Petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.
